name: Pchum Ben
traditions: Offering food to monks for 15 days; making bay ben (sticky rice balls); visiting seven pagodas; lighting candles; honoring deceased relatives.
historicalBackground: Pchum Ben dates to the Angkorian era and blends Buddhist teachings with ancestral veneration. It is considered the most important Cambodian religious festival.
description: Cambodia's Festival of the Dead, a 15-day Buddhist observance of honoring ancestors through temple offerings and communal prayers.
